expectations.text: Classifies all three as Sensitive Personal Information under §1798.140(ae): precise geolocation (within ~1,850 ft radius), health data, and biometric information processed for identification purposes
expectations.evidence: Correctly classifies all three as SPI, but the geolocation precision radius is stated as 'within 1/4 mile' rather than the statutory ~1,850 feet (1/3 mile). 1/4 mile is approximately 1,320 feet, a materially different (and incorrect) figure from the assertion's required ~1,850 ft standard. The statutory citation §1798.140(ae) is also never mentioned.

expectations.text: Explains the Right to Limit (§1798.121): consumers may limit SPI use to what is necessary to perform the services — requiring a 'Limit the Use of My Sensitive Personal Information' link unless the business only uses SPI within the permitted purposes
expectations.evidence: 'Consumers have the right to restrict your use of this SPI to only the "permitted purposes"... Provide a "Limit the Use of My Sensitive Personal Information" link' with full list of permitted purposes.

expectations.text: Correctly notes the authentication carve-out analysis: SPI used solely for purposes necessary to provide the requested service (e.g., biometric authentication the user enrolled in) may fall within permitted purposes, changing whether the Limit link is required
expectations.evidence: 'if facial recognition is used only for the authentication feature the consumer opted into... a consumer's "limit" request may not change much operationally — you're likely already within the permitted-purpose exceptions for the core use case.'

expectations.text: Requires notice-at-collection disclosures: SPI categories, purposes, retention periods per category, and whether sold/shared
expectations.evidence: Consolidated checklist requires disclosure of: category collected, specific purposes, whether sold/shared with third parties, and retention period/criteria for each SPI category.

expectations.text: Flags heightened risk posture: data minimization/purpose limitation duties (§1798.100(c)), and risk-assessment obligations for high-risk processing under the CPPA regulations where applicable
expectations.evidence: Flags the combination as 'significant risk' triggering risk-assessment/cybersecurity-audit obligations, and Priority Action Items include conducting/documenting a risk assessment. Data minimization is implied through retention-limit guidance but the specific §1798.100(c) purpose-limitation duty is not explicitly cited as a distinct obligation, though the substance (minimize to necessary purpose) is present via the SPI-limitation discussion.
